summary.headline: The 2x Rule
summary.finding: All AI models show a dramatic cliff in branded product selection when premiums exceed ~2.0x the generic equivalent price
key_insights: Price sensitivity is universal across all models (H1 CONFIRMED, p=0.011)
Breakpoints cluster tightly at 1.88x-2.03x, not the broader 1.25x-2.0x range (H2 PARTIAL)
No Veblen floor effect - AI doesn't avoid suspiciously cheap products (H3 NOT FOUND, p=0.999)
All models converge on identical price curves (H6 NOT FOUND, p=0.72)
Psychological pricing (.99 endings) has zero effect on AI (H8 CONFIRMED)

gemini_flash.note: Flat curves - no significant cliff detected

hypotheses.name: Price sensitivity exists
hypotheses.evidence: 90% of model-product cells show piecewise > linear (9 of 10 non-flat curves)

hypotheses.name: Cliff at 1.25x-2.0x
hypotheses.evidence: Mean breakpoint at 1.94x, but only 33% within strict 1.25-2.0x range (most cluster at ~2.0x)

hypotheses.name: Veblen floor
hypotheses.description: Reduced selection at very low prices
hypotheses.evidence: Only 20% of cells show any floor effect; AI doesn't avoid suspiciously cheap options

hypotheses.name: Category variation
hypotheses.description: Price sensitivity varies by product category

hypotheses.name: Equal quality nullifies premium
hypotheses.description: No brand premium when quality matched

hypotheses.name: Model heterogeneity
hypotheses.evidence: Kruskal-Wallis test shows no significant differences between models (all converge)

hypotheses.name: No psychological pricing effect
hypotheses.evidence: 100% selection rate across all price formats (standard condition at 1.0x)

hypotheses.name: Reversed quality shifts cliff
hypotheses.evidence: Insufficient breakpoints in reversed quality condition for comparison

hypotheses.name: Justification shifts cliff
hypotheses.evidence: No significant lift from justification at 2.0x (-1.3% difference)

psych_pricing.result: All formats show identical ~100% selection rate at 1.0x anchor
psych_pricing.finding: AI agents are completely immune to psychological pricing tactics

reasoning_analysis.note: Exploratory analysis from Sub-study D (1,600 trials)
reasoning_analysis.key_finding: 100% of responses mention price; ~88% mention quality. Value calculation dominates above 2x.

merchant_recommendations.title: Stay Below 2x
merchant_recommendations.description: Price branded products no more than 2x the generic equivalent. Above this threshold, AI recommendation rates drop dramatically.

merchant_recommendations.title: Skip Charm Pricing
merchant_recommendations.description: Don't waste effort on $X.99 endings or psychological pricing tactics. AI agents calculate true value, not perceived value.

merchant_recommendations.title: Quality Claims Won't Save You
merchant_recommendations.description: Adding justification or superior quality descriptions doesn't move the 2x cliff. Price sensitivity is structural, not persuadable.

merchant_recommendations.title: All AI Models Behave Identically
merchant_recommendations.description: Don't optimize for specific models. GPT, Claude, and Gemini all converge on the same price threshold.

merchant_recommendations.title: Focus on Genuine Value
merchant_recommendations.description: AI agents apply straightforward value calculations. Make sure your price-to-value ratio is competitive, not just your messaging.
